evidence: import { gsap } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/gsap@3.15.0/index.js" — version-pinned CDN ES module import with no SRI mechanism available for ESM imports
description: Vanilla example imports GSAP and ScrollTrigger from a public CDN via ES module import; pinned version avoids unpinned-dependency risk but ESM imports have no SRI option, so copy-pasting this file into production loads third-party code on every page load
suggested_fix: Add a comment noting this pattern is for zero-build demo purposes only; production apps should npm install gsap and bundle it.

evidence: "postinstall": "nuxt prepare" — nuxt prepare is Nuxt's own documented CLI command that generates local .nuxt type stubs; no network call, no shell/eval, no file writes outside the project's own .nuxt directory
description: package.json postinstall hook matches the generic postinstall-script pattern but runs Nuxt's standard, documented lifecycle command rather than arbitrary or obfuscated code
fp_reason: nuxt prepare is a well-known, non-networked, local-only lifecycle command shipped by the nuxt dependency itself, not arbitrary third-party code execution
rule_gap: SEC-postinstall-script should allowlist well-known benign framework lifecycle commands (nuxt prepare, husky install, etc.) instead of flagging on the mere presence of a postinstall key
